title: Drift Profile
description: Unified profile format covering static call-tree analysis (current) and future runtime sampled/evented modes. Modeled on speedscope, pprof, V8 CPU profile, and JetBrains dotTrace Call Tree.

schema_version.description: Profile-format schema version, MAJOR.MINOR. Bump major on breaking change.

mode.description: static = derived from source without execution (this tool today). sampled = pprof/V8-style stack samples. evented = speedscope-style open/close frame events.

entries.description: One tree per entry point. For static mode each entry is a root the user pinned (e.g. an HTTP handler).

frames.description: Optional interned frame table (speedscope-style). When present, CallTreeNode.frame_id references into it. When absent, CallTreeNode carries name/file/line inline.

string_table.description: Optional pprof-style string interning. Index 0 MUST be the empty string when present.

source_root.description: Absolute path of the project root analyzed

Summary.description: Top-level rollup. Mirrors PyCharm's Statistics tab + dotTrace overview.

languages.description: Languages encountered in the source root.

symbols.description: Total defined symbols across the project.

edges.description: Total directed edges in the call graph.

duration_ns.description: Wall-clock time of the analysis itself (not the analyzed program). Optional.

categories.description: Aggregate category counts across all entries.

top_callers.description: Symbols ranked by fan-in (incoming call count).

top_callees.description: Symbols ranked by fan-out (outgoing call count).

hot_paths.description: Chains from an entry to a categorized leaf. Like PyCharm's 'Most affected' but statically derived.

dead_code.description: Symbols with no callers AND not pinned as entries.

recursive_symbols.description: Symbols in an SCC of size > 1 (mutual recursion).

findings_by_kind.description: Count of structured findings per kind across the project. Same shape as `categories`. See $defs/FindingKind for the enum.

findings_top.description: Top-N findings as `(node_id, kind, severity, line)` triples, sorted by severity DESC. The viewer resolves `node_id` via its existing call-tree node index.

roots_overview.description: Per-entry-point rollup: subtree share, categories reached, findings by severity, first callees, callers. Mirrors pprof's `top -cum` at root granularity.

entry_declarations.description: Declared entry points harvested from both container-deployment files (Dockerfile, docker-compose) and per-language package manifests (package.json scripts/bin/main, deno.json tasks, pyproject.toml `[project.scripts]`, Cargo.toml `[[bin]]`). Each entry is optionally linked back to the in-graph symbol it most likely launches. See `EntryKind` for the closed-set of source flavors.

sql_files_scanned.description: Count of `.sql` files drift parsed in this run. `null` when the pass was disabled (`--no-sql-files`); `0` means scanned, none could be parsed. The matching synthetic `CallTreeNode`s appear in `entries[]` carrying `entry_labels: ["sql:file"]`.

sql_files_with_findings.description: Count of `.sql` files that produced at least one finding. Pair with `sql_files_scanned` for the trust-contract line 'scanned N, M had problems'.

frames.description: Ordered frame labels from entry to leaf.

Frame.description: Interned function/symbol descriptor. Mirrors speedscope Frame and pprof Function.

CallTreeNode.description: One node in the displayed call tree. Maps to PyCharm/dotTrace Call Tree rows and V8 ProfileNode.

frame_id.description: If `frames` table is provided, index into it. Otherwise omit.

file.description: Path relative to the analyzed project root.

depth.description: 0 = entry point, increments per nesting level.

subtree_size.description: Total reachable symbol count from this node (proxy for total time).

callers.description: Inverse edges. Lets the UI render PyCharm's 'callers' panel.

category_self.description: If this node itself touches a categorized resource (e.g. session.add → db).

categories_reached.description: Aggregated category counts over this node's transitive subtree.

external_calls.description: Library / framework calls that don't resolve to a defined symbol in the project.

self_value.description: Runtime-only: self cost in this node (excludes callees). Unit = profile.unit.

total_value.description: Runtime-only: cost of this node + its subtree. Unit = profile.unit.

sample_count.description: Runtime-only: sample hits on this frame's top of stack.

complexity.description: McCabe cyclomatic complexity of this symbol's body (decision points + 1).

loc.description: Lines of code in this symbol's body.

nesting_depth.description: Maximum nesting level inside the body.

call_site_count.description: Total invocations of this symbol anywhere in the project (not unique callers).

is_recursive.description: True if this symbol is in a strongly-connected component of size > 1.

pagerank.description: PageRank score over the directed call graph (Brin-Page, alpha=0.85).

n_plus_one_risk.description: True if a db/cache call is invoked inside a loop within this symbol's body. Derived from `findings` (kind=n_plus_one) when present.

blocking_in_async.description: True if is_async and a db/network/io call is invoked outside an await expression. Derived from `findings` (kind=blocking_in_async) when present.

findings.description: Structured, severity-scored findings attached to this node. Replaces (and supersedes) the per-node booleans above; the booleans stay populated as derived convenience values.

entry_labels.description: Human-readable entry-declaration labels (e.g. `Dockerfile CMD`, `compose:api command`, `package.json:scripts.start`, `pyproject:scripts.cli`) attached when this symbol is the resolved target of an `EntryDecl`. See `Summary.entry_declarations` for the source records.

ExternalCall.description: An unresolved reference (third-party / stdlib) that the catalog classified.

receiver.description: Object name before the call, when present (e.g. `session` in `session.add(order)`).

in_loop.description: True if the call site is inside a loop AST node (signals N+1 candidate).

in_await.description: True if the call site is inside an await_expression.

sql_literal.description: Captured SQL text for known SQL-sink calls (e.g. `cursor.execute(...)`, `sqlx::query!(...)`). Optional; absent for non-SQL calls. Consumed by the SQL Query Analyzer (sql_lint.rs).

Category.description: Resource category. `compute` is the default for everything that doesn't touch an external resource.

Sample.description: Runtime-only: pprof/V8-style stack sample. Not used in static mode.

stack.description: Frame ids, root → leaf (or leaf → root if `stack_order = leaf_first`).

values.description: One value per profile.value_types entry (e.g. cpu_ns, alloc_bytes).

ValueType.description: pprof-style value column descriptor. Runtime mode only.

Severity.description: Finding severity. Bumped by impact signals (subtree reach, pagerank, call-site count) via `bump_by_impact`.

Effort.description: Rough fix-cost estimate. Composes with severity into 'immediate fixes' (high × trivial/small) and 'refactor candidates' (large or multi-finding clusters). Inspired by SonarQube's remediation-time signal.

FindingKind.description: Closed-set finding kind. New kinds are additive; viewers ignore unknown values.

Evidence.description: One concrete artifact supporting a finding. Usually a call site; `call` may also be a sentinel like `import` or `loop` for non-call evidence.

Finding.description: Structured, severity-scored issue attached to a CallTreeNode. The node provides the context (file, parent_class, metrics); this object provides the issue.

effort.description: Rough fix-cost estimate. Defaults to `medium` if omitted (older fixtures).

line.description: Call-site or relevant line within the symbol — NOT the symbol-start line.

FindingTopRef.description: Pointer into the call tree for `Summary.findings_top`. `node_id` matches `CallTreeNode.id`.

RootOverview.description: Per-entry-point rollup. One row per `Report.entries[i]`, derived from data already on `CallTreeNode`.

callers.description: In-graph callers of this root. Empty for true entry points (HTTP handlers, main).

first_callees.description: Up to 5 direct callees — `where does this root go first?`

ImmediateFix.description: Quick-win row in `Summary.immediate_fixes` — a finding that's high severity AND trivial/small effort.

EntryDecl.description: A declared entry point parsed from a container-deployment file (Dockerfile / docker-compose) or a language package manifest (package.json, deno.json, pyproject.toml, Cargo.toml). The profiler treats these as first-class roots alongside in-graph symbols because they ARE the processes the system actually launches.

file.description: Path to the source manifest, relative to the analyzed root.

raw.description: Joined argv / value as written in the source file.

argv.description: Parsed argument list. JSON-array form is split per element; shell form is whitespace-split. Single-file manifests (`main`, `bin`, `[[bin]].path`) emit a single token.

service.description: Logical name attached to this entry. For compose: the service name (`services.<name>`). For package.json `scripts`/`bin` (object form): the command name. For pyproject scripts: the script name. For Cargo `[[bin]]`: the binary `name`.

EntryKind.description: Closed-set tag identifying which manifest produced an `EntryDecl`. New variants are additive; older viewers ignore unknown values.

EntryMatch.description: Back-link from an `EntryDecl` to the in-graph symbol the matcher believes the entry point launches. `confidence`: `exact` (argv referenced a file we parsed, or pyproject `pkg.mod:func` resolved to a named function), `likely` (module/convention match like `python -m pkg.mod`).

RefactorCandidate.description: Symbol-level aggregate in `Summary.refactor_candidates` — this whole unit needs a serious look, not a one-line patch.
